task_completion.visual_evidence: The main Juris-M application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Initiate the file import process in Juris-M.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Import' dialog box or file picker window launched from Juris-M is visible.

task_completion.description: Select the correct RIS file for import.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file picker window shows the file 'supreme_court_cases.ris' selected within the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the import created a new collection in the library.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new collection or folder named 'supreme_court_cases' (or similar matching the imported filename) is visible in the left pane of the Juris-M window.

task_completion.description: Verify the legal references are populated in the Juris-M library.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The center pane of the Juris-M window displays a list of imported items (Supreme Court cases) corresponding to the RIS file.

integrity.description: The agent used the Juris-M graphical user interface to perform the import.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with Juris-M menus (e.g., File -> Import) and dialog boxes, rather than terminal commands modifying the SQLite database directly.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Juris-M application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application window has the standard Juris-M (or Zotero-style) interface, icons, and layout, not a text editor or mocked interface.

integrity.description: The agent imported the file rather than manually creating items.
integrity.visual_evidence: Evidence of the import dialog or an automatically named collection folder ('supreme_court_cases'), rather than the 'New Item' manual entry dialog being used repeatedly.
